serving.config_yaml: # extra_llm_api_options — gpt-oss-120b (MXFP4 MoE) on DGX Spark (playbook standard shape). # free_gpu_memory_fraction is a kv_cache_config key, enable_padding a cuda_graph_config key # (TRT-LLM 1.2.1 rejects them as top-level LLM args). disable_overlap_scheduler: true kv_cache_config: free_gpu_memory_fraction: 0.8 # CUDA graphs DISABLED on GB10 (sm_121a). The MXFP4 MoE autotunes fine (FP4 Blackwell path, # unlike qwen's bf16 MoE), but CUDA-graph warmup for batch_size=4 SEGFAULTS # ("Run generation-only CUDA graph warmup for batch size=4 ... !!! Segfault encountered !!!"). # Running eager avoids the graph-capture crash; throughput is lower but inference is correct, # which is what the runtime bridge cell needs. cuda_graph_config: null

model_metadata.role: external agentic anchor + second 4-bit MoE (OpenAI open model, MXFP4, Blackwell-tuned) — replaces DeepSeek as model #3; lets us separate model effects from FP4-on-Blackwell effects alongside Nemotron NVFP4

sanity_check.prompt: Write a Python function `nth_prime(n)` that returns the n-th prime number (1-indexed), with a short docstring and three example assertions. Then explain in two sentences how it works.
sanity_check.error: generation failed: HTTP Error 400: Bad Request

fairness_note: Runtime is TensorRT-LLM (vs vLLM for gpt-oss/qwen baselines). Quality metrics are model-driven and comparable across runtimes; throughput/energy/latency are runtime-dependent and must be read with the qwen/gpt-oss dual-runtime bridge. seed, temperature, and the OpenCode version are held identical to the vLLM runs.
